title: Study in Italy: 2026–2027 Scholarships for International Students at Università degli Studi ‘G. d’Annunzio
summary: Apply now for fully supported scholarships at Università degli Studi “G. d’Annunzio” Chieti-Pescara for the 2026–2027 academic year. International students, including those enrolling in Joint Programmes or INGENIUM Pathways, are invited to apply. Overview of the Scholarships The University offers 15 scholarships for international students , including 5 specifically for students in Joint Programmes or INGENIUM Pathways . Scholarships cover the full duration of the degree programme and provide both financial and academic support. Eligible Degree Programmes include: Bachelor in Economics and Finance Master in Psychology of Well-Being and Performance Master in Planetary Sciences Master in Health Professions of Rehabilitation Sciences Master in Building Engineering – Engineering Management of Cultural Heritage Master in Computational Cognitive Science Master in Economics and Management Master in Legal Sciences for Internationalisation and Business Innovation Financial Support and Additional Benefits Each scholarship provides: €6,000 gross financial contribution Full exemption from University fees , except for the regional fee (€140) and stamp duty (€16) Additional support services include: Orientation and integration support for international students Access guidance for the University online system (MYPAGE Ud’A) Personalized reception, psychological support, and teaching materials for students with disabilities or specific learning difficulties Italian language courses Important: Scholarships cannot be combined with grants from other institutions, including ADSU Chieti-Pescara, MAECI, CRUI, foreign governments, or private organizations. Eligibility Criteria Must hold a foreign qualification recognized for admission to the selected degree programme. Bachelor applicants require at least 12 years of schooling (special provisions exist for 10- or 11-year systems). Master applicants must hold a foreign academic qualification granting access to Master’s programmes . Note: Transfers from other Italian universities are not allowed. Application Process Register online Complete the registration and select: “Evaluation test → Registration for the assessment test call for applications 15 scholarships for international students 2026/27 a.y.” Application period: 12 March – 20 April 2026, 23:59 (Italian time) Required Documents: Valid passport/ID Curriculum Vitae Motivation letter (in English) Degree programme selection form (Annex 2) English language certificate (B2 level for programmes taught in English) Academic qualifications and official transcripts Declaration of Value or ENIC-NARIC Statement of Comparability Note: Documents not in Italian must include a certified translation and legalization/Apostille. Selection Procedure Applications are assessed by a University Committee based on academic qualifications (60 points) and CV/motivation letter (40 points) . A final ranking will be published on the University portal. Tie-breakers: younger candidates first, then female candidates if needed. Maintaining the Scholarship Bachelor’s Degree: Students must acquire: Year 2: 30 ECTS by 31 October Year 3: 95 ECTS by 31 October Master’s Degree: Students must acquire: Year 2: 48 ECTS by 31 October Scholarship payments are made in installments upon meeting ECTS requirements. Failure to meet the required credits may result in suspension or revocation. Acceptance, Renunciation, and Revocation Candidates must accept or waive the scholarship within 10 days of notification. Scholarship holders requiring a visa must follow the official visa procedure: Universitaly – International Students Withdrawal from studies or transfer to another university will revoke the scholarship immediately. Personal Data Processing The University processes personal data in compliance with EU GDPR 2016/679 and relevant Italian legislation. Data is used solely for managing the scholarship application and related procedures. More details: Privacy Information – UniCh This announcement is published in the University Register, on the University website, and across its social media channels for maximum visibility. Discover more Scholarship opportunities here. The post Study in Italy: 2026–2027 Scholarships for International Students at Università degli Studi ‘G. d’Annunzio appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Join QUNO as a Programme Assistant in Geneva – Human Impacts of Climate Change & Human Rights & Refugees 2026-2027
summary: Are you an emerging professional passionate about international affairs, human rights, or climate action? The Quaker United Nations Office (QUNO) in Geneva is now accepting applications for Programme Assistant positions for the 2026–2027 term. These roles provide a unique opportunity for young professionals to gain hands-on experience within the UN system while working under the guidance of Quaker values and principles. Candidates can choose to support either the Human Impacts of Climate Change (HICC) programme or the Human Rights Refugees (HRR) programme, both central to QUNO’s mission of advancing peace, justice, and sustainable development. About the Roles QUNO’s Programme Assistants play an integral part in supporting international advocacy and research initiatives. The HICC Programme Assistant will assist the Programme Representative in linking climate policy at the UN with human rights and local action, helping to promote ethical, science-based, and human-centered approaches to climate change. Responsibilities include research, drafting reports and briefing notes, facilitating meetings, and contributing to QUNO’s communications and social media channels. Similarly, the HRR Programme Assistant will support work related to human rights in the context of migration, conscientious objection, and international human rights advocacy. This includes attending and reporting on UN meetings, preparing materials for the UN Human Rights Council, assisting with the Quaker UN Summer School, and supporting QUNO’s broader human rights initiatives. Both roles combine programme support with administrative responsibilities, such as event coordination, database maintenance, technical assistance, and contributing to QUNO’s office culture and community. Why This Opportunity Matters QUNO is at the intersection of Quaker values and multilateral diplomacy. These Programme Assistant positions offer emerging professionals a rare opportunity to gain practical insight into UN processes, environmental governance, and human rights advocacy . Working in a small, collaborative team, Programme Assistants contribute directly to advancing QUNO’s work in climate justice, human rights, and sustainable economic systems. The roles also provide an immersive introduction to international advocacy, research, and diplomacy , with exposure to UN bodies, civil society networks, and diplomatic missions. Candidates develop transferable skills in research, writing, communication, and programme management while learning how ethical and values-driven approaches can influence global policy. What Participants Will Gain Hands-on experience in international diplomacy and UN advocacy , observing and contributing to real-world policy discussions. Professional and career development , including writing reports, briefing papers, and managing projects within key UN processes. N etworking opportunities with UN professionals, diplomats, NGOs, and civil society organizations. Experience in programme coordination , including involvement in the Quaker UN Summer School and broader office initiatives. Who Can Apply Candidates should be recent graduates or early-career professionals with an active interest in international affairs, climate policy, or human rights, and a strong alignment with Quaker values. Applicants must have an undergraduate degree or equivalent, excellent English communication skills, and the ability to work collaboratively in a small team. Familiarity with UN systems, French or another UN language, or previous relevant experience is an advantage but not essential. Due to Swiss immigration requirements, applicants must either: be nationals of Switzerland or an EU/EFTA state, or for exceptional candidates from certain partner countries (e.g., USA, Canada, Australia), QUNO may assist in applying for a work visa, or already hold legal permission to work in Switzerland for the contract duration. Programme Location and Duration Geneva, Switzerland 31 August 2026 – 30 August 2027 Gross monthly salary: CHF 4,262 (approx. CHF 3,600 net after deductions) Benefits include a return travel ticket, limited French language course budget, 27 days paid holiday, and support with accommodation at the Grand Morillon residence. Application Process and Deadline To apply, candidates must complete the specific Programme Assistant application form for the role they are interested in: HICC Programme Assistant : Email completed form to qunojob2@quno.ch with subject line “Programme Assistant, HICC.” HRR Programme Assistant : Email completed form to qunojob1@quno.ch with subject line “Programme Assistant, HRR.” Applications must be submitted by Friday, 8 May 2026 . Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews during the week of 25 May 2026. Candidates are encouraged to structure their personal statement around commitment to Quaker values, analytical and communication skills, and how their experience aligns with the programme’s focus. Learn more and apply on the official job posting. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Apply Now: TikTok Platform Manager Role at WFP (UN Job Opportunity in Italy)
summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) is currently recruiting a TikTok Platform Manager to join its global communications team in Rome, Italy. This is an exciting opportunity for digital media professionals with strong expertise in vertical video content and social media strategy to contribute to one of the world’s leading humanitarian organizations. About the World Food Programme The World Food Programme is the largest humanitarian organization globally , dedicated to saving lives in emergencies and supporting communities in building sustainable futures. As a 2020 Nobel Peace Prize Laureate , WFP operates in multiple countries, delivering food assistance and driving long-term solutions to hunger, conflict, and climate-related challenges. WFP fosters a diverse, inclusive, and high-performing workforce guided by its core values: Integrity, Collaboration, Commitment, Humanity, and Inclusion . Job Overview Position: TikTok Platform Manager Location: Rome, Italy Contract Type: Consultant Duration: 11 months Work Type: Full-time Department: Communications (COM/HQ) The selected candidate will lead WFP’s TikTok presence and support the organization’s broader vertical video strategy across platforms such as Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts. Key Responsibilities Platform Management Strategy Manage and grow WFP’s TikTok account with engaging, high-quality content Develop and execute a vertical video strategy across multiple platforms Monitor trends, algorithm updates, and emerging features Provide guidance to regional and country offices on content creation Test new platforms and recommend strategic investments Content Creation Production Produce and edit short-form video content using tools like TikTok and CapCut Serve as a visible representative of WFP on social platforms Adapt existing content into engaging vertical video formats Collaborate with global teams to align messaging and storytelling Community Engagement Monitor comments and engage audiences through replies, duets, and stitches Support community-building efforts and manage brand interactions Escalate reputational risks or sensitive issues when necessary Analytics Reporting Analyze performance metrics and audience behavior Provide monthly reports on growth, engagement, and content performance Identify trends and best practices to improve digital strategy Content Resource Management Build and maintain a library of reusable video assets Develop templates and toolkits for global teams Coordinate with regional offices to source relevant content Expected Deliverables By the end of the contract, the Platform Manager is expected to: Successfully implement WFP’s vertical video strategy Increase TikTok audience growth and engagement Develop a comprehensive content library Deliver regular analytics and performance reports Provide training and guidance on video content creation Eligibility Criteria Education Degree in Communications, Digital Media, Marketing, Journalism, or a related field Experience Minimum 2–3 years of experience managing social media platforms Proven success in creating high-performing or viral video content Experience with NGOs, international organizations, or humanitarian agencies is an advantage Skills Competencies Strong knowledge of TikTok and video editing tools (e.g., CapCut, InShot, Splice) Excellent storytelling and visual content skills Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment Strong organizational and time management skills Proactive, strategic, and able to work independently Languages Fluency in English is required Knowledge of another UN language (French, Spanish, or Arabic) is an asset Why Join WFP? Working with WFP offers: A chance to contribute to a global humanitarian mission A diverse, inclusive, and multicultural work environment Opportunities for professional development and career growth Collaboration with passionate teams across global offices Competitive compensation packageApplication Process Applicants must submit their applications through WFP’s official online recruitment system. Required documents at the initial stage include: Updated CV Cover Letter Additional documents may be requested later in the recruitment process. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Application Deadline 18 April 2026 (23:59 CET) APPLY HERE Important Notes WFP does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process Applications submitted via email or external platforms will not be considered Candidates must ensure their application profiles are complete and accurate The TikTok Platform Manager role at the World Food Programme is a unique opportunity for digital content professionals to apply their skills in a meaningful, global context. If you are passionate about storytelling, social media innovation, and humanitarian impact, this position offers a rewarding and dynamic career path. Apply now to become part of a team working toward a world with zero hunger. Discover more similar opportunities here. title: Fully Funded UN Youth Scholarship in Geneva: Chemicals and Waste Study Programme 2026
summary: The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR), in collaboration with the Chemicals and Waste Youth Platform (CWYP), is offering a fully funded scholarship for young people to participate in the United Nations Chemicals and Waste Study Programme in Geneva, Switzerland. This opportunity is designed for youth aged 18 to 35 who are interested in environmental governance, sustainability, and international policymaking. About the Opportunity This programme will take place from June 23 to July 17, 2026, in Geneva, a global hub for diplomacy and international cooperation. It combines participation in the UNITAR Chemicals and Waste Study Programme with real-time engagement in major multilateral processes. Selected participants will have the opportunity to attend and contribute to the Basel Convention Open-Ended Working Group (OEWG-15) as well as the 62nd session of the United Nations Human Rights Council. The study programme itself includes expert-led lectures, interactive workshops, and site visits to key international institutions such as the Palais des Nations, the World Health Organization, and the International Labour Organization. Participants will explore critical topics such as hazardous waste management, chemical safety, public health, environmental governance, and international regulatory frameworks. In addition to structured learning, the programme offers direct exposure to how global environmental agreements are negotiated and implemented. Participants will engage with policymakers, observe negotiations, and contribute youth perspectives to ongoing discussions, making this a highly immersive experience in international governance. Why This Opportunity Matters This programme plays a critical role in bringing youth voices into global environmental governance. Issues related to chemicals and waste have direct impacts on public health, ecosystems, and sustainable development, especially in vulnerable regions. By equipping young leaders with knowledge, networks, and direct experience in policymaking, the programme helps build the next generation of professionals who can contribute to global solutions. It also promotes more inclusive participation in international decision-making processes, ensuring that perspectives from the Global South and underrepresented communities are heard. What You’ll Gain his opportunity offers both financial and professional benefits. Selected participants will receive full funding, including economy-class round-trip flights, hotel accommodation, and programme fees. In addition, meals, visa costs, and local transportation expenses will be reimbursed. This ensures that participants from diverse backgrounds can fully engage in the programme without financial barriers. Beyond funding, participants gain valuable hands-on experience in international diplomacy. They will attend high-level meetings, observe real negotiations, and interact with UN professionals, government representatives, and civil society organizations. This exposure helps participants better understand how global decisions are made and how they can contribute to them. The programme also provides significant career development opportunities. Participants receive a UNITAR Certificate of Completion and gain insights into career pathways within the United Nations and related international organizations. They will also build a global network of peers and professionals working in sustainability, environmental policy, and public health. Eligibility Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old at the time of application. They must be from a developing country, least developed country, small island developing state, or represent Indigenous communities. Applicants must be available to travel to Geneva and participate in the full programme from June 23 to July 17, 2026. A demonstrated interest in environmental issues, sustainability, or international policy is important, along with a clear motivation to apply the knowledge gained in their communities or professional work. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must complete the official application form provided by the organizers. The application includes written responses that demonstrate motivation, relevant experience, and how participation aligns with future goals. Applicants are expected to provide thoughtful, original responses. The deadline to apply is April 27, 2026, at 23:59 UTC. Decisions are expected in mid-May 2026. To learn more and apply, view the official application form. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: WINGS Africa Coordinator Job Opportunity 2026: $30,000–$40,000 Global Role in Philanthropy Network Development (Remote in Africa)
summary: The WINGS Africa Coordinator position is a high-impact global opportunity for experienced professionals passionate about strengthening philanthropy ecosystems across Africa. Hosted by WINGS (Worldwide Initiatives for Grantmaker Support), this role is designed to support regional engagement, collaboration, and innovation in philanthropy while contributing to global development goals. This full-time consultancy role offers professionals the chance to work with a global network of over 200 organizations across more than 60 countries, shaping the future of philanthropy and social impact across Africa and beyond. About WINGS WINGS is the only global network dedicated to philanthropy support and development organizations. It brings together a diverse community of thought leaders, practitioners, and changemakers committed to advancing giving, collaboration, and systemic social change. The organization focuses on: Strengthening philanthropy ecosystems globally Promoting collaboration and shared learning Supporting organizations that enable effective giving Advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Through its work, WINGS aims to create a more just, equitable, and sustainable world by empowering philanthropy actors and networks. Overview of the Africa Coordinator Role The Africa Coordinator will play a central role in advancing WINGS’ strategic priorities in the region, particularly in nurturing networks and strengthening philanthropy ecosystems. Key Role Details Position Type: Full-time consultancy (40 hours per week) Location: Must be based in Africa Salary Range: USD 30,000 – 40,000 annually Deadline: April 17, 2026 The role sits within the Network and Ecosystems Team and reports directly to the Network and Ecosystems Director. Core Objectives of the Role The Africa Coordinator position is structured around three main objectives: 1. Network Growth and Engagement The Coordinator will: Serve as a primary contact for WINGS members in Africa Support engagement in global and regional activities Facilitate regional conversations through the Africa Working Group Design and implement online and in-person events Identify and onboard new members Strengthen collaboration across regions 2. Strengthening Philanthropy Support Ecosystems The role will contribute to building strong, sustainable philanthropy systems by: Coordinating ecosystem strengthening initiatives Identifying capacity-building needs Supporting collaboration among stakeholders Connecting regional and global actors 3. Advancing WINGS’ Mission in Africa The Coordinator will support broader organizational goals by: Contributing to research and knowledge production Supporting communications and fundraising Promoting partnerships and collaboration Representing WINGS at regional and global events Key Responsibilities The Africa Coordinator will undertake a wide range of strategic and operational responsibilities, including: Developing and implementing regional project plans Coordinating Africa Working Group activities Organizing webinars, meetings, and conferences Preparing proposals and reports Supporting fundraising initiatives Identifying partnerships and collaboration opportunities Producing research, reports, and knowledge resources Monitoring regional trends and innovations Managing project timelines, budgets, and deliverables Maintaining project documentation Representing WINGS in key forums and events Eligibility and Requirements Candidates must meet the following qualifications: Educational Background Bachelor’s degree (required) Master’s degree or equivalent experience (preferred) Professional Experience Minimum of 7 years of relevant experience Experience in nonprofit project or network coordination Experience in philanthropy or related sectors (highly desirable) Skills and Competencies Strong interpersonal and communication skills Ability to build relationships across diverse stakeholders Experience in fundraising and proposal writing Strong writing and analytical skills Proficiency in digital tools and database systems Global mindset and cultural awareness Language Requirements Fluency in English and French (required) Knowledge of Portuguese (advantage) Additional Requirements Must be based in Africa Willingness to travel up to 15% Understanding of African philanthropy and civil society landscape Compensation and Benefits The position offers: Annual compensation of USD 30,000 – 40,000 Opportunity to work with a global network Exposure to international philanthropy ecosystems Leadership and strategic experience Contribution to global development and SDGs Application Process Applicants must submit their application through the Official Online Form. Key Application Details Deadline: April 17, 2026 Applications reviewed on a rolling basis Early application is strongly encouraged Why You Should Apply This role offers a unique opportunity to: Shape philanthropy ecosystems across Africa Work with global leaders and organizations Lead impactful regional initiatives Build partnerships and influence policy Contribute to sustainable development Learn more about the WINGS Africa Coordinator position HERE. Discover more Job opportunities on OFY. title: Apply Now: University of Oslo Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in Global Life-Course Criminology
summary: Are you an early-career researcher passionate about understanding crime through a global, comparative lens? The University of Oslo (UiO) is offering an exciting Postdoctoral Research Fellowship as part of the groundbreaking Life-Course Trajectories to Crime Globally (CRIMLIFE) project, funded by the European Research Council (ERC). This fully funded position provides a unique opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research across multiple continents while collaborating with leading scholars in criminology and social sciences. About the Fellowship The Postdoctoral Research Fellow (SKO 1352) will work closely with Professor Sveinung Sandberg at the Department of Criminology and Sociology of Law. The CRIMLIFE project explores how life experiences and social contexts shape criminal trajectories across different regions of the world. Location: Oslo, Norway Duration: 3 years (research only), or 4 years (including 25% teaching/administration) Start Date: Spring 2026 Salary: NOK 595,000 – 680,000 annually (depending on experience) About the CRIMLIFE Project CRIMLIFE aims to advance global comparative life-course criminology by combining life histories and life-course analysis to better understand crime and desistance. Key Focus Areas: Role of state, family, employment, and culture in shaping criminal behavior Comparative research across six countries : Norway, Czech Republic, Türkiye, Nigeria, India, and Colombia Mixed-methods approach including: 300 participants 900 qualitative life-story interviews Large-scale survey data The project addresses critical gaps in criminology, which has traditionally focused on Western contexts, by bringing in perspectives from the Global South and underrepresented regions. Research Responsibilities The selected candidate will: Conduct global comparative qualitative research across multiple countries Write and publish academic research articles Participate in data coding, analysis, and organization Support project management and reporting Contribute to ethical approvals and data management plans Assist in operating TSD (Services for Sensitive Data) Engage in international collaboration and dissemination activities You will also actively participate in seminars, workshops, and collaborative research with an international team. Eligibility Criteria Required Qualifications: PhD (or equivalent) in: Criminology Sociology Psychology Political Science Or a related social science field Doctoral thesis submitted by application deadline and approved before appointment Strong command of English (written and spoken) Knowledge of research ethics processes Preferred Qualifications: Experience with: Life-history interviews Narrative analysis Life-course criminology Familiarity with any of the project countries or regions Interest or experience in comparative qualitative research Personal Skills Ideal candidates should demonstrate: Strong independent research ability Excellent collaboration and communication skills Initiative and motivation to work in an international, multidisciplinary team Structured and organized working style Benefits Working at the University of Oslo offers: A dynamic and inclusive academic environment Strong opportunities for career growth and development Access to a global research network Up to 1.5 hours of weekly exercise during work hours Generous pension scheme and welfare benefits Support for developing teaching qualifications (if required) Why Apply? This fellowship is ideal for researchers looking to: Work on a high-impact, globally relevant project Gain international fieldwork experience Collaborate with leading experts in criminology Contribute to innovative, interdisciplinary research shaping the future of crime studies Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Cover letter outlining motivation and qualifications Project proposal (4–7 pages) including: Research objectives Methodology Ethical considerations Alignment with CRIMLIFE goals Updated CV Academic transcripts and certificates Up to three academic publications Contact details for at least two referees (Optional) Doctoral dissertation assessment Note: All documents must be in English or a Scandinavian language. Click Here , to apply. Application Deadline 30th April 2026 About the University of Oslo The University of Oslo (UiO) is Norway’s oldest and highest-ranked university, with over 26,500 students and 7,200 employees. The Department of Criminology and Sociology of Law is internationally recognized for its excellence in research, teaching, and academic contribution. If you re passionate about understanding crime through a global, interdisciplinary perspective and want to be part of a pioneering research initiative, this fellowship is a perfect opportunity. Apply now and contribute to redefining global criminology research. title: Apply Now: Building Nonprofit Advocacy Capacity 2026 (Free Online Program for Nonprofits)
summary: The Building Nonprofit Advocacy Capacity Program 2026 is a free, part-time, and fully virtual training opportunity designed to help U.S.-based public interest organizations strengthen their policy and advocacy impact. This program is ideal for nonprofits, direct service providers, and academic or research centers seeking to develop effective advocacy strategies , influence policy, and expand their organizational capacity in the policy space. Participants will gain practical tools to: Educate and persuade stakeholders on policy issues Build partnerships that drive social change Secure resources (including staffing) to support advocacy work Strategically integrate policy advocacy into their organization Program Structure The course runs over 5 days and combines expert-led sessions with practical assignments. Participants are expected to actively engage and complete coursework, including a final assignment . Format: Virtual Duration: 5 days (May 11–15, 2026) Daily Sessions: 2 hours (9–11 AM PT / 12–2 PM ET) Cost: Free Participants will also receive: Cohort-based learning and peer collaboration Feedback from program staff Potential individualized feedback on final assignments (free or small fee depending on cohort size) Program Timeline Application Opens: February 26, 2026 Final Application Deadline : April 13, 2026 (11:59 PM PT) Notification of Acceptance: By April 20, 2026 Program Dates: May 11–15, 2026 Eligibility Criteria To be eligible, applicants must: Work with a U.S.-based public interest organization (e.g., nonprofit, university center, direct service provider) Have limited experience in policy and advocacy work Be interested in building or strengthening an advocacy arm within their organization Be at least 21 years old Live in or be legally able to work in the United States Commit to attending all 5 virtual sessions Priority will be given to: Registered nonprofit organizations Organizations new to policy and advocacy work May also be considered (space permitting): Public benefit corporations Labor unions Who Should Not Apply Organizations that already have full-time policy or advocacy staff Individuals not currently affiliated with an organization Previous participants of the December 2025 cohort What You’ll Learn This program introduces a practical framework for advocacy, helping participants: Understand how policy change works Build advocacy strategies with limited resources Expand organizational capacity for policy engagement Transition from direct service to policy impact How to Apply Interested applicants should carefully review eligibility requirements and program expectations before submitting their application. Early applications are encouraged for faster review . APPLY NOW The Building Nonprofit Advocacy Capacity Program 2026 is a valuable opportunity for emerging advocacy leaders to gain the skills, strategy, and confidence needed to influence policy and create lasting social impact—without financial barriers. If your organization is ready to take the next step into policy and advocacy work, this program provides the foundation to make that transition successfully. Discover many more similar opportunities by clicking Here. title: Bseisu-University of Cambridge Scholarship Opportunity for Middle Eastern Students (2026–27)
summary: The University of Cambridge , in partnership with the Amjad Suha Bseisu Foundation , is offering two highly competitive fully funded scholarships for the 2026/27 academic year. These scholarships are designed to support outstanding students from the Middle East in pursuing a full-time Master’s degree at one of the world’s leading universities. This scholarship is open to applicants from Lebanon, Jordan, Palestine, and Syria, and is tenable at any college within the University of Cambridge. While candidates from all academic disciplines are eligible, priority is given to those pursuing STEM-related fields, reflecting a strong emphasis on advancing scientific and technical expertise in the region. The program is part of Cambridge’s broader commitment to increasing access to world-class education for talented students from underrepresented and conflict-affected regions. By removing financial barriers, this scholarship enables scholars to fully engage in academic life and contribute meaningfully to their fields. Why This Opportunity Matters Access to high-quality education at leading global institutions remains limited for many students in the Middle East due to financial and structural barriers. This scholarship directly addresses that challenge by providing full funding and access to one of the world’s most prestigious universities. By supporting students from Lebanon, Jordan, Palestine, and Syria, the program contributes to building future leaders, researchers, and professionals who can drive positive change in their communities and beyond. The emphasis on STEM fields also aligns with global priorities in innovation, technology, and sustainable development. However, by remaining open to all disciplines, the scholarship ensures a diverse and inclusive academic cohort. What You’ll Gain This scholarship provides comprehensive financial support, covering international tuition fees, living expenses, visa costs, and the immigration health surcharge. This ensures that recipients can focus entirely on their studies without financial stress. Beyond funding, scholars gain access to the exceptional academic environment at Cambridge. This includes world-class faculty, cutting-edge research facilities, and a global network of peers and professionals. The experience equips students with advanced knowledge, critical thinking skills, and research capabilities that are highly valued across industries and academia. In addition, being part of Cambridge opens doors to long-term career opportunities, leadership roles, and global influence. Graduates often go on to work in international organizations, research institutions, and leadership positions in both public and private sectors. Scholarship Features The Bseisu Cambridge Scholarship offers full financial coverage for selected candidates. It includes payment of international tuition fees, a maintenance allowance to support living expenses, and coverage of visa-related costs, including the immigration health surcharge. Only two scholarships are awarded each year, making this a highly selective and prestigious opportunity. Selection is based on both academic merit and financial need, ensuring that support reaches students who demonstrate excellence and require financial assistance. A key feature of this scholarship is its flexibility. It is tenable at any college within Cambridge and applicable to a wide range of Master’s programs, giving students the freedom to pursue their academic interests. Eligibility To be eligible, applicants must be citizens of one of the following countries: Jordan, Lebanon, Palestine, or Syria. Candidates must apply for a full-time Master’s degree at the University of Cambridge and meet the academic requirements of their chosen program. Applicants must be classified as overseas students for fee purposes. While all fields of study are eligible, priority is given to those pursuing STEM disciplines. Selection is based on a combination of academic excellence and financial need. This means applicants should demonstrate strong academic performance as well as a clear case for financial support. You can see the full list of schools and courses that qualify for the scholarship on the official post. Application Process and Deadline There is no separate application required for this scholarship. Candidates only need to apply for admission to a Master’s program at the University of Cambridge. To be considered, applicants must submit their course application before the funding deadline specific to their chosen program. These deadlines vary by course and are listed in the Postgraduate Course Directory on the university’s official website. All eligible applicants who meet the criteria will automatically be considered for the scholarship. The selection process takes place between March and July 2026. Applicants who are not selected may not receive individual notifications, so it is important to monitor updates carefully. 👉 To learn more and apply, visit the official posting of the scholarship. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Apply now: Astra AI Safety Fellowship 2026 in Berkeley
summary: The Astra Fellowship, offered by Constellation Institute , is a highly competitive, fully funded program designed to accelerate the next generation of leaders in AI safety. Hosted in Berkeley, California, this five-month, in-person fellowship brings together talented individuals from diverse backgrounds to work on some of the most pressing challenges in artificial intelligence. As AI systems become more powerful and widely deployed, ensuring their safety, alignment, and governance has become a global priority. The Astra Fellowship responds to this need by connecting fellows with leading researchers, policymakers, and strategists working at the forefront of AI safety. Fellows are embedded within a vibrant research ecosystem where over 300 AI safety professionals collaborate regularly. Through structured mentorship, research support, and hands-on project work, participants contribute directly to real-world AI safety initiatives across empirical research, strategy, and governance. The program also collaborates with leading organizations in the field, including OpenAI , further expanding opportunities for fellows to engage with high-impact work and networks. Why This Opportunity Matters Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming industries, economies, and societies worldwide. However, with this transformation comes significant risk, particularly if systems are not designed and governed responsibly. The Astra Fellowship plays a critical role in addressing this challenge by building a pipeline of skilled professionals dedicated to AI safety. By equipping fellows with the tools, knowledge, and networks needed to tackle complex risks, the program contributes to shaping a safer and more responsible AI future. Globally, there is a growing demand for individuals who can bridge the gap between technical innovation and ethical responsibility. Programs like Astra help ensure that the development of AI technologies is aligned with human values and long-term societal wellbeing. What You’ll Gain The Astra Fellowship offers a rare combination of financial support, mentorship, and career acceleration. Fellows receive a monthly stipend of $8,400, allowing them to fully dedicate their time to research and professional development without financial pressure. In addition, empirical research fellows benefit from approximately $15,000 per month in compute resources, enabling them to work on advanced AI projects that require significant technical infrastructure. Participants gain direct access to leading experts in AI safety, benefiting from weekly mentorship sessions, collaborative research environments, and ongoing feedback. This exposure helps fellows rapidly develop both technical and strategic skills. Beyond the fellowship itself, Astra provides strong career outcomes. Over 80 percent of previous fellows have transitioned into full-time roles in AI safety at top organizations such as Google DeepMind , Anthropic , and OpenAI . Others have launched their own initiatives with support from the program’s incubation resources. Eligibility The Astra Fellowship is open to individuals from a wide range of academic and professional backgrounds. Applicants do not need prior experience specifically in AI safety, making this an accessible opportunity for those transitioning from related fields. Strong candidates typically demonstrate a clear interest in reducing risks associated with advanced AI and bring relevant technical or domain-specific expertise. This may include experience in areas such as computer science, public policy, governance, research, or other related disciplines. The program is particularly suited for individuals who want to transition into AI safety careers or start their own projects addressing emerging challenges in the field. International applicants are welcome, and visa support is available for selected fellows. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the Astra Fellowship, candidates must complete the application through the official Constellation website. Applicants are required to submit their application through the online portal, where they will find detailed instructions and guidelines on how to complete each section. It is important to carefully follow all steps and provide accurate information to ensure your application is considered. The application deadline is May 3, 2026, at 11:59 PM (Anywhere on Earth). Late applications are not accepted, so candidates are encouraged to apply well in advance. The selected cohort will begin the fellowship in September 2026. 👉 To learn more and apply, visit the official Constellation Astra Fellowship page . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: African Economic Scholars Program 2026: AESP 4th Cohort
summary: The African Economic Scholars Program (AESP) 2026 has officially opened its call for applications, inviting outstanding young scholars across Africa to join its highly competitive 4th cohort. This prestigious opportunity is designed to identify, train, and empower the next generation of African economists and policy thinkers who will shape the continent’s future. About the African Economic Scholars Program (AESP) The African Economic Scholars Program is a competitive fellowship initiative aimed at nurturing academically excellent and policy-driven young Africans with strong interests in economics and social sciences. The program focuses on building analytical capacity, research competence, and policy engagement among emerging scholars. Participants in the program typically gain access to: Advanced academic and policy training Mentorship from leading economists and policy experts Networking opportunities with peers and professionals Exposure to real-world economic policy challenges in Africa The 2026 cohort marks the fourth intake, continuing the program’s commitment to strengthening Africa’s intellectual and policy leadership pipeline. Who Should Apply? This fellowship is specifically designed for young African scholars who demonstrate academic excellence and a strong interest in economic policy and research. Target Audience The program is ideal for: Final-year undergraduate students Recent graduates Early-career scholars in economics or related fields Eligible Countries Applicants must be nationals or residents of one of the following countries: Nigeria Ghana Kenya South Africa These countries are specifically highlighted for the 2026 cohort, meaning applicants outside these locations are not eligible for this cycle. Academic Requirements and Qualifications To qualify for the African Economic Scholars Program 2026, applicants must meet all of the following criteria: 1. Field of Study Applicants must have studied or be currently studying one of the following: Economics Political Science Other Social Science-related disciplines This ensures that participants have a foundational understanding of economic systems, governance, and policy frameworks. 2. Academic Level Applicants must fall into one of these categories: Final-year undergraduate students Recent graduates This requirement ensures that candidates are at a critical transition point into careers or advanced academic pursuits. 3. Academic Performance Applicants must demonstrate strong academic achievement: Must have graduated or be on track to graduate with: First Class Honors, or Second Class Upper Division This reflects the program’s emphasis on academic excellence and intellectual rigor. 4. Graduation Timeline Applicants must meet the recency requirement: Must not have graduated before 2025 This ensures that all participants are part of a recent academic cohort and are still early in their professional journeys. Key Benefits of the AESP Fellowship Although specific benefits may vary, fellows of the African Economic Scholars Program typically gain: 1. Advanced Training Participants receive structured learning experiences designed to deepen their understanding of economic theory, applied research, and policy analysis. 2. Mentorship Scholars are paired with experienced economists, academics, and policymakers who provide guidance and career support. 3. Professional Networking The program connects fellows with a community of like-minded scholars and professionals across Africa and beyond. 4. Career Development Participants are equipped with skills that enhance their competitiveness for: Graduate school admissions Policy roles Research positions International development careers Why This Fellowship Matters Africa faces complex economic challenges that require highly skilled analysts, researchers, and policymakers. The AESP aims to build a pipeline of young leaders who are equipped to: Influence economic policy Conduct impactful research Contribute to sustainable development across the continent By participating in this program, scholars position themselves at the forefront of Africa’s intellectual and economic transformation. How to Prepare for a Strong Application To maximize your chances of selection: Highlight your academic achievements clearly Demonstrate interest in economic policy or research Showcase leadership, initiative, or extracurricular involvement Prepare a compelling personal statement if required Ensure all academic records are accurate and up to date Apply here Deadline 19th April 2026 The African Economic Scholars Program 2026 is more than just a fellowship—it is a transformative opportunity for young African scholars to gain world-class exposure, build influential networks, and contribute meaningfully to economic development across the continent.
